channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Amid years of war, these Israelis are still pushing for peace
item.dc:creator: Lior Soroka
item.description: As Israel’s longest period of sustained armed conflict drags on, the country’s diminished peace movement finds itself struggling to be heard on the sidelines.

item.title: U.S. and Iran to close deal within a day, Trump says, but Tehran yet to confirm
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw, Rachel Chason, Cate Cadell
item.description: An agreement would extend the ceasefire and pave the way for a longer truce, leaders said, though an Iranian official denied a deal will be signed Sunday.

item.title: Global HIV prevention declined drastically after Trump aid cuts, U.N. finds
item.dc:creator: Rael Ombuor
item.description: Cuts from the U.S. and others have hit hard, with the number of people receiving PrEP dropping by 38% from 2024 to 2025, according to data from 62 countries.

item.title: U.S. and Iran close to signing ceasefire deal, officials say
item.dc:creator: Adam Taylor, Susannah George, Karen DeYoung
item.description: After days of uncertainty and tit-for-tat military strikes, "text of the peace deal has been reached,” according to Pakistan's prime minister.

item.title: Pope Leo had to catch a ride from the king of Spain after plane’s engine failed
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: The pope wrapped up a trip to Spain by borrowing a private jet from King Filipe VI after the Vatican's chartered plane, with the press corps on board, broke down.

item.title: China and Myanmar detain U.S. citizens in separate incidents, sparking alarm
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Tan
item.description: Despite no immediate evidence that they were connected, the detentions of two vocal commentators on Myanmar triggered alarm in the business and research communities.
